State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 837 · SACRAMENTO, 95801 · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

June 24, 1976


Alan G. Campbell
Judge of the Municipal Court
Los Angeles Judicial District
4200 Parva Avenue
Los Angeles, California 90027

Re: Penalty for Late Filing
of Campaign Statement

Dear Judge Campbell:

We have reviewed your request for a Commission opinion
that, under the circumstances relating to your late filing of
a candidate's campaign statement, you should not be assessed
a late filing penalty. Although we are sympathetic to your
position and find no reason to believe that you willfully dis-
regarded the requirements of the Political Reform Act of 1974,
we are not in a position to alter the Registrar-Recorder of
Los Angeles County's assessment of a $100 late filing penalty
against you.

Government Code Section 91013(a) permits the filing
officer, in this case the Registrar-Recorder of Los Angeles
County, to waive a late filing penalty if he determines that
the late filing was not willful and the enforcement of the
liability will not further the purposes of the Act, but only
if the late filing is made "within five days after the fil-
ing officer has sent specific written notice of the filing
requirement."

There is no allowance in the statute for the situation
you described where you did not receive notice of your cam-
paign statement's delinquency until after the expiration of
the five-day grace period. Therefore, the Registrar-Recorder
appears to be without authority to waive the fine in your case
even though, on the facts, a waiver would appear to be an
equitable solution.

Please know that the Commission is constantly striving
to remedy any inequities that might be caused by provisions

The image appears to be mostly blank or extremely faint, with only some barely visible marks at the very top that are too unclear to transcribe with certainty. I can see what might be partial curved shapes or marks, but they are not legible enough to transcribe accurately according to your requirements.

Alan G. Campbell
June 24, 1976
Page Two

of the Political Reform Act. It has proceeded in this effort
through the adoption of interpretive regulations. It also
proposed a number of statutory changes earlier this year
for the same general purpose. Yours will become one of the
situation-examples we regularly collect so that remedial
actions can be taken through statutory changes.

By a copy of this letter, we are informing the Registrar-
Recorder of the County of Los Angeles of our position on your
request.

Sincerely,

Michael Bennett
Executive Director

MB:MEM:ma

cc: Registrar-Recorder
County of Los Angeles

[The image appears to be almost entirely blank/white with only a few small dots or marks visible at the top, but no clearly legible text to transcribe.]

IN CHAMBERS

MUNICIPAL COURT
LOS ANGELES JUDICIAL DISTRICT
ALAN G. CAMPBELL, JUDGE

June 10, 1976

Fair Political Practices Commission
107 South Broadway, Room 21408
Los Angeles, California 90012

Attention: Mr. John C. Greenwood

Gentlemen: .

I enclose a copy of my Interim Campaign State-
ment covering the period from April 27 through May 24, 1976 with
respect to my candidacy for re-election to the Office of Judge of
the Municipal Court of the Los Angeles Judicial District. I also
enclose a copy of my letter with which that statement any my
check for $100 penalty, paid and submitted under protest, was
transmitted to the Registrar-Recorder of Los Angeles County and
I enclose as well my declaration that I was unablebefoce June 9,
1976 to execute such Interim Campaign Statement.

I respectfully request the Commission's opinion that
under the circumstances stated no penalty upon me attach or that
under the circumstances such penalty matter, should be and is
waived and its payment excused.

A copy of this letter and of the enclosures are being
sent to the attention of Ms. Natalie West in your office in Sacramento.
If further information from me or further action by me should be in-
dicated, I shall appreciate your advice to such effect.

Respectfully submitted,

ALAN G. CAMPBELL,

Enclosures
cc: /Ms. Natalie West

The image appears to be almost entirely blank or white with only some very faint marks or artifacts visible at the top and a few scattered dots/marks. There is no clearly legible text content to transcribe from this document image.

DECLARATION OF ALAN G. CAMPBELL:

I left Los Angeles and the United States on Sunday, May 16, 1976 and

was thereafter out of the State of California and the United States and con-

tinuously in the United Kingdom or the Republic of Ireland until about 12:00 P.M.

on Saturday, June 5, 1976. On the following days, Sunday, June 6 and Monday,

June 7, 1976, I was ill, and I was unable to return, and did not return to my

office in the Los Angeles County Courthouse until Tuesday, June 8, 1976.

Late in the day on June 8, 1976 I found in the mail accumulated since my

departure a notice from the Registrar-Recorder of Los Angeles County, dated

June 1, 1976 that an interim Candidate's Campaign Statement should, within

five days thereafter be filed with him covering the period of April 27 to May

24, 1976.

I was unable to execute any such statement before June 9, 1976 under

penalty of perjury within California or within any other State. Additionally I

had no knowledge before June 9, 1976 as to whether or not any committee

supporting my re-election to the Office of Judge of the Municipal Court of

the Los Angeles Judicial District had received or spent any money, made or

received any loans or pledges, or incurred any expenses. On June 9, 1976

I ascertained that before May 25, 1976 there had been no such occurrences.

Upon ascertaining the facts I immediately executed an Interim Candidate's

Campaign Statement, and such is filed coincidentally with the filing and

submission of this declaration and of my request that the Fair Political

The image provided appears to be almost entirely blank or very faintly marked, with only minimal visual elements visible at the top that are too unclear to transcribe accurately. The page contains mostly white space with what may be some very faint marks or artifacts, but no legible text content that can be reliably transcribed.

Declaration of Alan G. Campbell

Page Two


Practices Commission render its opinion that I am not liable for the payment

of any penalties for late filing under Government Code 91013, or otherwise.

I declare under penalty of perjury that the above statements are

true.

Executed on June 9, 1976 at Los Angeles, California.


____________________________
ALAN G. CAMPBELL

The image appears to be a blank or nearly blank page with only some faint marks or artifacts at the very top. There is no clearly legible text content to transcribe.

IN CHAMBERS

MUNICIPAL COURT
LOS ANGELES JUDICIAL DISTRICT
ALAN G. CAMPBELL, JUDGE

June 10, 1976

Mr. Leonard Panish
Registrar-Recorder
County of Los Angeles
808 North Spring Street
Los Angeles, California 90012

Dear Sir:

I enclose my Candidate's Campaign Statement covering the
period from April 17, 1976 through May 24, 1976 with regard to my
candidacy for re-election as a Judge of the Municipal Court for the
Los Angeles Judicial District, with a copy thereof.

I also enclose, submitted under protest, my check to you for
$100 as the calculated penalty for filing such statement after five days
from your notice to me in this connection dated June 1, 1976.

I also enclose a counter part of my declaration that I was unable
to execute such statement before June 9, 1976, and a copy of my letter
of this date to the Fair Political Practices Commission in which I ask
for an opinion that neither such penalty nor any part thereof is lawfully
due, and that in any event such penalty should be waived and its pay-
ment excused.

I am sending a copy of all of the enclosures to the attention of
Ms. Natalie West in the Commission's office at 1100 K Street, Sacramento,
California 95814.

I hope that this matter will receive proper attention by all concerned,
under the advice of respective legal counsel, and that the enclosed $100
will finally be refunded to me.

If further information from me or action by me should be indicated,
I shall appreciate your advice to such effect.

Yours very truly,


ALAN G. CAMPBELL

Enclosures
cc: Ms. Natalie West

committees in connection with the making of expenditures. You control a committee if you, your agent or any other committee you
control has significant influence on the actions or decisions of the committee.)
